title: Implementing Erlang/OTP Rate Limiter at Zendesk
situation: Zendesk’s ticketing ingestion path was vulnerable to overload during traffic spikes, risking service degradation.
task: Protect the ingestion pipeline with a robust, low-latency rate-limiting mechanism.
action: Implemented an Erlang/OTP-based rate limiter using process-based counters and backpressure primitives.
result: Successfully guarded ingestion path during peak loads (no specific metric provided).

title: Designing CRDT Sync Engine for Canva’s Real-Time Collaboration
situation: Canva needed a scalable, low-latency sync mechanism for real-time design collaboration across millions of concurrent users.
task: Develop a CRDT-based sync engine with a gRPC streaming API to support seamless multi-user editing.
action: Designed and implemented the design-collaboration CRDT sync engine in Rust, using Protocol Buffers and gRPC streaming.
result: API used by 90 million monthly active users.

title: Migrating Bitbucket Audit Logs from Cassandra to ScyllaDB
situation: Bitbucket’s audit log store was running on Cassandra, incurring high storage costs and slow tail read latencies.
task: Improve cost-efficiency and read performance for audit log storage without sacrificing reliability.
action: Led migration of the audit log store from Cassandra to ScyllaDB, optimizing schema and tuning read/write paths.
result: Reduced storage cost by 41% and tail read latency by 63%.

title: Building Elixir Presence Fabric for Confluence Launches
situation: Confluence required real-time presence awareness during major product launches, demanding support for high-concurrency websocket sessions.
task: Design and implement a resilient presence system capable of sustaining peak concurrent sessions.
action: Built an Elixir/Phoenix presence fabric leveraging Erlang/OTP supervision trees for fault tolerance and horizontal scaling.
result: Sustained 2.1 million concurrent websocket sessions during peak load.

title: Rearchitecting Jira Activity-Stream Ingestion in Rust
situation: The Jira activity-stream ingestion service was experiencing high latency (p99: 380ms) under a 4 billion events/day Kafka firehose, impacting real-time user experience in Jira.
task: Reduce end-to-end latency for activity-stream ingestion while maintaining reliability and scalability.
action: Rearchitected the service in Rust using the Tokio async runtime, optimizing data flow and leveraging low-level performance controls.
